expectations.text: Response demonstrates n() combined with .scale("C:minor") or similar
Response explains that n() numbers are scale degrees where 0 is the root
expectations.evidence: 'The numbers in n() represent positions (degrees) in the scale. In C minor, 0 maps to C (the root)'

expectations.text: Response indicates that any integer can be used and it will stay in scale
expectations.evidence: 'I'm not fully certain how large numbers or negative numbers behave — they may wrap around octaves, but I'd verify in the docs' — the response hedges and does not affirm that any integer works
